Title: A conserved strategy of chalcone isomerase-like protein to rectify promiscuous chalcone synthase specificity

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Multiple land plants (e.g., Antirrhinum majus, Glycine max, Arabidopsis thaliana, Ipomoea nil, Physcomitrella patens, Marchantia polymorpha, Selaginella moellendorffii, Ginkgo biloba, Oryza sativa, Hydrangea macrophylla, Vitis vinifera)
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Antirrhinum majus; Glycine max; Arabidopsis thaliana; Ipomoea nil; Physcomitrella patens; Marchantia polymorpha; Selaginella moellendorffii; Ginkgo biloba; Oryza sativa; Hydrangea macrophylla; Vitis vinifera
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0; Ipomoea nil NA; Glycine max cv. Enrei for some cloned materials; Antirrhinum majus cv. Maryland Orange / Montego Red in metabolon assays; others NA
Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Multiple land plants; Arabidopsis; Escherichia coli
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Antirrhinum majus; Glycine max; Physcomitrella patens; Marchantia polymorpha; Escherichia coli
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 and chil-3 mutant; snapdragon cvs Maryland Orange and Montego Red; NA otherwise
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonoids, especially THC-derived chalconoids and downstream proanthocyanidins/anthocyanins/flavonols
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and UV-screening roles; support growth, survival, reproduction, and defense; anthocyanins contribute flower color; flavonols aid co-pigmentation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type IV chalcone isomerase-like protein; CHI-fold protein family; non-catalytic CHI-like protein
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: CHIL genes are ubiquitous in land plant genomes and expressed along with CHS genes in diverse plant species
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: AtCHIL promoter active in Arabidopsis seed-related complementation context; petal cell context in snapdragon; engineered E. coli co-expression context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances flavonoid accumulation by binding CHS and rectifying its promiscuous catalysis, increasing THC/chalconoid formation and reducing CTAL; required for normal proanthocyanidin accumulation in Arabidopsis seed coat
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; co-precipitation; crosslinking; biolayer interferometry; in vitro CHS product assays; Arabidopsis chil mutant complementation; engineered Escherichia coli co-expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHIL is a conserved non-catalytic component of flavonoid metabolons that physically interacts with CHS across land plants. 2. CHIL does not markedly increase total polyketide production, but rectifies CHS specificity by promoting THC/chalconoid formation and suppressing CTAL. 3. AtCHIL fully complements the Arabidopsis chil-3 seed proanthocyanidin defect, whereas distant bryophyte CHILs do not, indicating species-specific functional compatibility.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CHS; FNSII (snapdragon); IFS-1; IFS-2; CHI in Arabidopsis and some species
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Heterologous CHIL from phylogenetically distant species may not fully complement in planta due to species-specific CHS compatibility; inappropriate promoter choice can limit complementation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arabidopsis seed phenotype context; in vitro assays at 30°C; E. coli fed with 1 mM p-coumaric acid; Nicotiana benthamiana agroinfiltration for BiFC
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: AtCHIL native promoter for Arabidopsis complementation; CaMV 35S mentioned as less suitable in prior study; T7 promoter in Escherichia coli co-expression assays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS in co-precipitation/product identification; no accession number reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful auxiliary factor for engineering higher flavonoid flux; should be paired with compatible species-specific CHS and native/appropriate promoter contexts to improve flavonoid traits such as seed PA or flower pigmentation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CHIL is not a transcription factor and lacks CHI catalytic activity; acts as a rectifier rather than a simple activator of CHS; proposed to recruit early flavonoid enzymes to metabolon sites as an additional species-specific function

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Multiple land plants (e.g., Antirrhinum majus, Physcomitrella patens, Selaginella moellendorffii, Ginkgo biloba, Oryza sativa, Arabidopsis thaliana, Glycine max, Hydrangea macrophylla, Vitis vinifera, Ipomoea nil)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Antirrhinum majus; Physcomitrella patens; Selaginella moellendorffii; Ginkgo biloba; Oryza sativa; Arabidopsis thaliana; Glycine max; Hydrangea macrophylla; Vitis vinifera; Ipomoea nil
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Antirrhinum majus cv. Maryland Orange / Montego Red for some assays; Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 for mutant context; Glycine max cv. Enrei for Gm4CL-3 source; others NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Multiple land plants; Escherichia coli for heterologous in vivo assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Antirrhinum majus; Physcomitrella patens; Selaginella moellendorffii; Ginkgo biloba; Oryza sativa; Arabidopsis thaliana; Glycine max; Hydrangea macrophylla; Vitis vinifera; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0/chil-3 for complementation context; Glycine max cv. Enrei for cloned Gm4CL-3; NA otherwise
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 2',4,4',6'-tetrahydroxychalcone (THC), assessed as THC/naringenin-derived chalconoids; downstream flavonoids/proanthocyanidins
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core entry step from phenylpropanoid pathway into flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 2',4,4',6'-tetrahydroxychalcone (THC); also forms p-coumaroyltriacetic acid lactone (CTAL) as byproduct
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses one p-coumaroyl-CoA with three malonyl-CoA units to form THC, but promiscuously also generates CTAL through derailment of the chalcone-forming pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first committed enzyme of flavonoid pathway; key control entry step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: CHS genes are expressed along with CHIL genes in diverse plant species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed coat context in Arabidopsis PA phenotype; petal context in snapdragon co-precipitation; engineered E. coli during p-coumaric acid feeding
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds (proanthocyanidins in Arabidopsis seed coat); petals/flowers for anthocyanin-flavonol context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids function in antioxidant and UV screening, plant survival, reproduction, and defense against biotic and abiotic stresses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces THC as the key precursor for flavonoids but also promiscuously forms CTAL; CHIL binding shifts CHS specificity toward THC/chalconoid production and away from CTAL
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assays with product LC-MS/HPLC quantification; kinetics; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; co-precipitation; biolayer interferometry; heterologous production in Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS from diverse land plants is intrinsically promiscuous, producing both chalconoids (THC/naringenin) and CTAL. 2. In the presence of homologous CHIL, CHS product specificity shifts strongly toward THC/chalconoids while total polyketide output changes only slightly. 3. CHIL–CHS interaction is conserved among land plants, but CHIL does not similarly affect related enzymes STS or CTAS.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without CHIL, substantial carbon flux is lost to CTAL byproduct formation, reducing flavonoid biosynthetic efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro standard assays with pH 7.5 at 30°C; E. coli feeding with 1 mM p-coumaric acid at 30°C for 3 h
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter in Escherichia coli heterologous assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS peptide/product analyses; no accession number reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Improving flavonoid production may require co-engineering CHS with compatible CHIL to minimize CTAL derailment and enhance metabolic flux into flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CHI generally did not affect CHS product specificity except species-specific interactions reported elsewhere; CHS monomer binds approximately one CHIL monomer
